content: Comparative performance analysis of intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) and random network distillation (RND) against generalized curious agents can serve as a diagnostic benchmark for how core agent memory architecture shapes autonomous exploration in 2024–2025 state-of-the-art active learning systems. This benchmark reveals that the exploration gap between traditional curiosity-driven methods and specialized curious agents emerges not from reward design alone, but from differences in how each agent architecture encodes long-term novel memory traces.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
